15MIN INCREMENTS
03
04
05
Scripts: Module 4
SCRIPTS
TO OFFER THE PRIZE
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 1 of 5 --

S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	G
OFFER
THE
PRIZE
John, the price is more than you want to pay, of course,
how could it ever be different, and it’s less than we want
to charge. The Blue Plastic Frogs are $1000 each, there’s no
way around that.
But it’s super interesting. You are going to meet—because
of the order size you have to place—you’re going to meet our
chief engineer.
This guy flies to Afghanistan twice a year to find the dye
that covers our product. Afghanistan is the only place in the
world with the right blue. So anyway, with the 1st delivery
I’m going to bring him along. He’s going to help with the
install...
This frames price as a deal
term that is fair for both of
you.
Always transition quickly
out of the intrigue ping
and get back on track of
moving the deal forward.
This is helpful in creating
open loops —threads of
conversation introduced
and then strategically cut
in order to keep attention.
Introduce your intrigue
ping like this. You can also
use a teasing hook to spike
interest:
“This reminds me of our
‘commando’ engineer...”
Frame Stacking Routine 1 -
Intrigue Frame & Price
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 2 of 5 --

S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	G
OFFER
THE
PRIZE
Now as you know, with this order of Blue Plastic Frogs you
need to get an irrigation report. These are $25,000. Now
fortunately in my previous life I was an irrigation consultant. I
have all the models and I still have the license.
But these reports are time-consuming. Although I sign
between 20 and 30 clients a month, I only do 5 irrigation
reports. I’ve already done four this month. But I think if I
look at my order report you’re going to be my 5th customer
and I’m going to do your irrigation report for you, because
you placed an order of this size.
I don’t really care about the money, but if you want to
work with me, this is the kind of stuff I bring to the table.
When you buy this order then you get me—that’s incredibly
valuable both economically and as a resource for your
business.
Frame Stacking Routine 1 -
Prize Frame & Terms
Very important throughout
the pitch process to
make their money and
commodity. This phrase is
particularly effective.
Identify for yourself
aspects of yourself and
your involvement that
are standout, first-class
features.
Obviously be sure that
this number makes sense
relative to where you are in
the month.
This introduces scarcity.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 3 of 5 --

S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	G
OFFER
THE
PRIZE
Frame Stacking Routine 1 -
Time Frame & Deadline
Everyone hates time
pressure in a sale situation,
so say that so both parties
understand that time
pressure is undesirable, yet
is not something that you
control.
Be sure to establish an
external motivator that has
nothing to do with you for
the deadline.
A quick note on credibility
and neediness:
If you tell a buyer that he
must place the order by X
time, and that time passes,
then you must have the
internal fortitude to refuse
that business. If you accept
the business after your
own deal term has been
swept aside, then you lose
ground and status, and
you also lose credibility
because your word means
nothing and has zero
consequences.
The blue plastic frogs are funny, especially this time of year.
Because this is when our inventories get low. The French
buy these things up. When our stock gets below 10,000, there
is a price trigger.
And the price goes up 33%. I’m just telling you right now that
you have about a week to finalize this order. Or a price increase
is going to get triggered, and you’re not going to be able to
come to me and say I want the previous price.
That is the real timeframe. And I’m not saying that you have
to buy today. Or tomorrow. I’m just telling you there is a real
world timeframe in which this deal has to get done.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 4 of 5 --

S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	G
OFFER
THE
PRIZE
Frame Stacking Routine 1 -
Moral Authority Frame & Counter
Pressure
Now that you have aligned
yourself with the forces
of good, apply counter
pressure.
Choose an aspect of your
service or product that
conveys moral authority,
i.e., social impact.
You choose what happens
or does not happen.
The blue plastic frogs that we manufacture are the best in the
world. They are 5-Goldstar, EEE-rated. They are the highest
environmentally rated in the world.
One problem is that we find companies buy our product
in order to demonstrate to regulators that they are
environmentally friendly.
But we do not allow companies to use us as a casual
demonstration of environmental friendliness. We have to
make sure that you guys are actually environmentally friendly
throughout.
So you have to prove to us what other environmental
programs you have running before we let you place an order
of this size. Because it’s important to us that our customers are
real and care about the environment and don’t just use a large
order with us to frame themselves as environmentally friendly.
So that’s something that you need to prove to us.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 5 of 5 --
